title: Maughania wangkae
intro: Maughania wangkae, also known as Wangkae, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Leguminosae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Wangkae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Wangkae is often used to make various preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and powder, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Maughania wangkae is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Maughania, which is a group of plants native to tropical regions. Maughania wangkae is further classified under the subfamily Faboideae and tribe Phaseoleae. The plant is closely related to other species in the same family and genus. Its classification as a member of the Fabaceae family makes it a legume, which is known for its nitrogen-fixing abilities.
